skill.name: Azure Monitor Ingestion SDK for Python
skill.description: Azure Monitor Ingestion SDK for Python. Use for sending custom logs to Log Analytics workspace via Logs Ingestion API.
skill.summary: Send custom logs to Azure Monitor Log Analytics workspace using the Logs Ingestion API.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only documentation (SKILL.md). No executable code was scanned. The skill provides reference documentation for the Microsoft Azure Monitor Ingestion SDK for Python. No security risks identified.

content.user_title: Send Custom Logs to Azure Monitor
content.value_statement: This skill enables Claude Code to send custom application logs to Azure Monitor Log Analytics workspaces using the official Logs Ingestion API. It provides code examples and best practices for integrating Azure monitoring into Python applications.
actual_capabilities: Upload custom logs to Azure Monitor Log Analytics workspace
Handle partial upload failures with error callbacks
Support both synchronous and asynchronous log ingestion
Configure Azure Government and sovereign clouds
Process batched log uploads with automatic compression
limitations: Requires valid Azure Data Collection Endpoint (DCE) and Data Collection Rule (DCR) configuration
Only supports JSON-formatted log data matching DCR schema
Does not create DCR or tables - these must be pre-configured in Azure

use_cases.title: Application Performance Monitoring
use_cases.description: Send application logs, metrics, and trace data from Python services to Azure Monitor for centralized logging and analysis.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers and site reliability engineers

use_cases.title: Security Audit Logging
use_cases.description: Ingest security events, authentication logs, and compliance data into Log Analytics for security monitoring and threat detection.

use_cases.title: Business Analytics Integration
use_cases.description: Push business metrics and operational data from Python applications to Log Analytics for custom reporting and dashboards.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Log Upload
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to use the Azure Monitor Ingestion SDK to upload custom logs to a Log Analytics workspace. Include the client setup with DefaultAzureCredential.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic log ingestion

prompt_templates.title: Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I handle partial upload failures when sending logs to Azure Monitor? Show an example with error callback and retry logic.

prompt_templates.title: Async Ingestion
prompt_templates.prompt: Write an example of using the async LogsIngestionClient to upload logs in a high-throughput Python application.

prompt_templates.title: Sovereign Cloud Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I configure the Azure Monitor Ingestion client for Azure Government cloud instead of public Azure?

output_examples.input: How do I upload logs to Azure Monitor?
output_examples.output: To upload logs to Azure Monitor, create a LogsIngestionClient with your Data Collection Endpoint and authenticate using DefaultAzureCredential. Then call client.upload() with your rule ID, stream name, and log data array.

The SDK supports Azure Identity libraries including DefaultAzureCredential, ManagedIdentityCredential, and ServicePrincipalCredential. DefaultAzureCredential automatically attempts multiple auth methods including managed identity, environment variables, and interactive login.

best_practices: Always include the TimeGenerated field in your log entries - it is required by Azure Monitor
Use the on_error callback to handle partial failures and implement retry logic for failed logs
Match your log schema to the Data Collection Rule column definitions to ensure proper ingestion
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode credentials in your application code - use Azure Identity
Do not skip error handling - network failures and partial uploads are common in log ingestion
Do not send logs without first validating they match your DCR schema - mismatched data will be rejected

faq.question: What is the difference between DCE and DCR in Azure Monitor?
faq.answer: A Data Collection Endpoint (DCE) is the ingestion URL where logs are sent. A Data Collection Rule (DCR) defines the schema, transformations, and destination table for your logs.

faq.question: Do I need to create the Log Analytics table first?
faq.answer: For custom tables, the table is automatically created when the DCR processes its first ingestion. Built-in tables must exist before data is sent.

faq.question: How does the SDK handle large log volumes?
faq.answer: The SDK automatically batches logs into 1MB chunks, compresses them with gzip, and uploads in parallel. You do not need to manually implement batching.

faq.question: Can I use this SDK with Azure Government?
faq.answer: Yes, configure the authority and credential_scopes for Azure Government or other sovereign clouds using AzureAuthorityHosts.

faq.question: What happens if some logs fail to upload?
faq.answer: The SDK provides an on_error callback that receives failed log entries. You can implement retry logic to resend those logs.

faq.question: Is async support available?
faq.answer: Yes, use LogsIngestionClient from azure.monitor.ingestion.aio with async/await patterns for high-throughput scenarios.
